evidence_quotes.quote: prevent video game publishers from destroying games.

paragraphs.text: Hello, Like many others, I would like to request for the Digital Fairness Act to include some form of protection that would prevent video game publishers from destroying games. Or in other words, leaving games in inoperable states after publishers choose to end support for the product. I find this practice disgusting not only because it means that a video game publisher is able to take away a customer's access to a product which they've already paid for, but also because I see it as destruction of art and culture. Many developers pour months or even years of their lives into creating games, and seeing the fruits of their labor almost entirely erased from existence is always heartbreaking. Especially since implementing end of life plans for video games isn't a demanding ask in the grand scheme of things. The Stop Destroying Videogames European citizens' initiative has already received over 1 400 000 signatures across the EU, which shows that people would welcome some form of regulation when it comes to the destruction of games, as long as it means that customers get to keep the games that they paid for. This needless destruction of products and art must end.
